explanation: This command represents a direct match to the Sigma rule's detection criteria, as it includes the use of ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe with an HTTP URL in the command line.
This command uses an FTP URL, which is also covered by the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It demonstrates a valid test case for the rule.
This command uses HTTPS, which is included in the Sigma rule's detection logic. It is a valid test case for verifying detection.
This command adds a hypothetical flag (-Url) to simulate a scenario where the URL is passed as a named parameter. It is a safe mutation for testing.
This command uses quotes around the URL, a safe insertion technique to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite formatting changes.
This command uses a double backslash in the URL path, a safe mutation to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite path normalization attempts.
This command omits the file extension from the URL, a safe omission technique to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite this change.
This command adds a verbose flag, a safe addition to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite additional arguments.
This command appends output redirection, a safe mutation to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite additional shell syntax.
This command adds standard error redirection, a safe mutation to test whether the Sigma rule can detect the command despite additional shell syntax.
